nist_800_53.title: Access Control
nist_800_53.description: Limit information system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate access control policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Account Management
nist_800_53.description: Define and document types of accounts; monitor use; manage creation, modification, disabling, and removal.

nist_800_53.title: Access Enforcement
nist_800_53.description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access.

nist_800_53.title: Information Flow Enforcement
nist_800_53.description: Enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system.

nist_800_53.title: Separation of Duties
nist_800_53.description: Separate duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion.

nist_800_53.title: Least Privilege
nist_800_53.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users.

nist_800_53.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
nist_800_53.description: Enforce a limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user.

nist_800_53.title: System Use Notification
nist_800_53.description: Display an approved system use notification message before granting access.

nist_800_53.title: Concurrent Session Control
nist_800_53.description: Limit the number of concurrent sessions for each system account.

nist_800_53.title: Device Lock
nist_800_53.description: Prevent further access by initiating a device lock after a period of inactivity.

nist_800_53.title: Session Termination
nist_800_53.description: Automatically terminate a user session after defined conditions.

nist_800_53.title: Permitted Actions without Identification or Authentication
nist_800_53.description: Identify user actions that can be performed without identification or authentication.

nist_800_53.title: Remote Access
nist_800_53.description: Establish and restrict remote access usage, configuration, and monitoring.

nist_800_53.title: Wireless Access
nist_800_53.description: Establish usage restrictions, configuration, and monitoring for wireless access.

nist_800_53.title: Access Control for Mobile Devices
nist_800_53.description: Establish usage restrictions, configuration, and monitoring for mobile devices.

nist_800_53.title: Use of External Information Systems
nist_800_53.description: Establish terms and conditions for use of external information systems.

nist_800_53.title: Information Sharing
nist_800_53.description: Enable authorized information sharing based on defined circumstances.

nist_800_53.title: Publicly Accessible Content
nist_800_53.description: Designate individuals authorized to make information publicly accessible.

nist_800_53.title: Awareness and Training
nist_800_53.description: Ensure that managers and users are made aware of the security risks and applicable requirements.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate awareness and training policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Literacy Training and Awareness
nist_800_53.description: Provide security literacy training to information system users.

nist_800_53.title: Role-Based Training
nist_800_53.description: Provide role-based security training to personnel with assigned security roles.

nist_800_53.title: Training Records
nist_800_53.description: Document and monitor individual information system security training activities.

nist_800_53.title: Audit and Accountability
nist_800_53.description: Create, protect, and retain information system audit records to enable monitoring, analysis, and reporting.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate audit and accountability policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Event Logging
nist_800_53.description: Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging.

nist_800_53.title: Content of Audit Records
nist_800_53.description: Ensure that audit records contain sufficient information to establish what events occurred.

nist_800_53.title: Audit Log Storage Capacity
nist_800_53.description: Allocate audit log storage capacity to meet organizational requirements.

nist_800_53.title: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
nist_800_53.description: Alert designated officials and take defined actions in the event of an audit processing failure.

nist_800_53.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
nist_800_53.description: Review and analyze system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity.

nist_800_53.title: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
nist_800_53.description: Provide audit record reduction and report generation capabilities.

nist_800_53.title: Time Stamps
nist_800_53.description: Use internal system clocks to generate time stamps for audit records.

nist_800_53.title: Protection of Audit Information
nist_800_53.description: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access.

nist_800_53.title: Non-Repudiation
nist_800_53.description: Provide protection against an individual falsely denying having performed a particular action.

nist_800_53.title: Audit Record Retention
nist_800_53.description: Retain audit records for a defined period of time consistent with records retention policy.

nist_800_53.title: Audit Record Generation
nist_800_53.description: Ensure that the information system generates audit records for defined events.

nist_800_53.title: Cross-Organizational Audit Logging
nist_800_53.description: Coordinate audit logging among external organizations for cross-organizational events.

nist_800_53.title: Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring
nist_800_53.description: Periodically assess security controls; authorize system operation; continuously monitor.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate assessment, authorization, and monitoring policy.

nist_800_53.title: Control Assessments
nist_800_53.description: Select and execute the appropriate assessors for security control assessments.

nist_800_53.title: Information Exchange
nist_800_53.description: Approve and manage the exchange of information between systems.

nist_800_53.title: Plan of Action and Milestones
nist_800_53.description: Develop and update a plan of action and milestones for the system.

nist_800_53.title: Authorization
nist_800_53.description: Assign a senior official as authorizing official; authorize the system to operate.

nist_800_53.title: Continuous Monitoring
nist_800_53.description: Develop a continuous monitoring strategy and implement monitoring.

nist_800_53.title: Penetration Testing
nist_800_53.description: Conduct penetration testing on the information system.

nist_800_53.title: Internal System Connections
nist_800_53.description: Authorize internal connections of system components.

nist_800_53.title: Configuration Management
nist_800_53.description: Establish and maintain baseline configurations throughout the system development life cycle.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate configuration management policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Baseline Configuration
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and maintain a current baseline configuration.

nist_800_53.title: Configuration Change Control
nist_800_53.description: Determine and document the types of changes that are configuration-controlled.

nist_800_53.title: Impact Analyses
nist_800_53.description: Analyze changes to the system to determine potential security and privacy impacts.

nist_800_53.title: Access Restrictions for Change
nist_800_53.description: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions for changes.

nist_800_53.title: Configuration Settings
nist_800_53.description: Establish and document configuration settings that reflect the most restrictive mode.

nist_800_53.title: Least Functionality
nist_800_53.description: Configure the system to provide only essential capabilities.

nist_800_53.title: System Component Inventory
nist_800_53.description: Develop and document an inventory of system components.

nist_800_53.title: Configuration Management Plan
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and implement a configuration management plan.

nist_800_53.title: Software Usage Restrictions
nist_800_53.description: Use software and associated documentation in accordance with contract agreements.

nist_800_53.title: User-Installed Software
nist_800_53.description: Establish policies governing the installation of software by users.

nist_800_53.title: Information Location
nist_800_53.description: Identify and document the location of information and where processing occurs.

nist_800_53.title: Signed Components
nist_800_53.description: Prevent the installation of software, firmware, and hardware components without verifiable signatures.

nist_800_53.title: Contingency Planning
nist_800_53.description: Establish, maintain, and implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate contingency planning policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Contingency Plan
nist_800_53.description: Develop a contingency plan that provides recovery objectives and restoration priorities.

nist_800_53.title: Contingency Training
nist_800_53.description: Provide contingency training to information system users.

nist_800_53.title: Contingency Plan Testing
nist_800_53.description: Test the contingency plan to determine its effectiveness.

nist_800_53.title: Alternate Storage Site
nist_800_53.description: Establish an alternate storage site including necessary agreements.

nist_800_53.title: Alternate Processing Site
nist_800_53.description: Establish an alternate processing site and initiate necessary agreements.

nist_800_53.title: Telecommunications Services
nist_800_53.description: Establish alternate telecommunications services including necessary agreements.

nist_800_53.title: Information System Backup
nist_800_53.description: Conduct backups of user-level and system-level information.

nist_800_53.title: Information System Recovery and Reconstitution
nist_800_53.description: Provide for the recovery and reconstitution of the system to a known state.

nist_800_53.title: Identification and Authentication
nist_800_53.description: Identify users, processes, and devices and authenticate those identities before granting access.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate identification and authentication policy.

nist_800_53.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
nist_800_53.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users.

nist_800_53.title: Device Identification and Authentication
nist_800_53.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate devices before establishing a connection.

nist_800_53.title: Identifier Management
nist_800_53.description: Manage system identifiers by receiving authorization and tracking assignment.

nist_800_53.title: Authenticator Management
nist_800_53.description: Manage system authenticators including initial content, verification, and refresh.

nist_800_53.title: Authentication Feedback
nist_800_53.description: Obscure authentication feedback during the authentication process.

nist_800_53.title: Cryptographic Module Authentication
nist_800_53.description: Authenticate to a cryptographic module that meets applicable federal standards.

nist_800_53.title: Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational Users)
nist_800_53.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate non-organizational users.

nist_800_53.title: Re-Authentication
nist_800_53.description: Require users to re-authenticate under defined circumstances.

nist_800_53.title: Identity Proofing
nist_800_53.description: Identity proof users that require accounts for logical access.

nist_800_53.title: Incident Response
nist_800_53.description: Establish an operational incident handling capability that includes preparation, detection, analysis, and recovery.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate incident response policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Incident Response Training
nist_800_53.description: Provide incident response training to information system users.

nist_800_53.title: Incident Response Testing
nist_800_53.description: Test the incident response capability using exercises and simulations.

nist_800_53.title: Incident Handling
nist_800_53.description: Implement an incident handling capability for security incidents.

nist_800_53.title: Incident Monitoring
nist_800_53.description: Track and document information system security incidents.

nist_800_53.title: Incident Reporting
nist_800_53.description: Require personnel to report suspected security incidents.

nist_800_53.title: Incident Response Assistance
nist_800_53.description: Provide an incident response support resource that offers advice and assistance.

nist_800_53.title: Incident Response Plan
nist_800_53.description: Develop an incident response plan that provides a roadmap for implementing incident response.

nist_800_53.title: Maintenance
nist_800_53.description: Perform periodic and timely maintenance of organizational information systems.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate maintenance policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Controlled Maintenance
nist_800_53.description: Schedule, perform, document, and review records of maintenance on system components.

nist_800_53.title: Maintenance Tools
nist_800_53.description: Approve, control, and monitor the use of information system maintenance tools.

nist_800_53.title: Nonlocal Maintenance
nist_800_53.description: Approve and monitor nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic activities.

nist_800_53.title: Maintenance Personnel
nist_800_53.description: Establish a process for maintenance personnel authorization and maintain a list of authorized personnel.

nist_800_53.title: Timely Maintenance
nist_800_53.description: Obtain maintenance support and spare parts for key system components within defined time periods.

nist_800_53.title: Media Protection
nist_800_53.description: Protect information system media, both digital and non-digital; restrict access to authorized individuals.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate media protection policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Media Access
nist_800_53.description: Restrict access to digital and non-digital media to authorized individuals.

nist_800_53.title: Media Marking
nist_800_53.description: Mark information system media indicating the distribution limitations and handling caveats.

nist_800_53.title: Media Storage
nist_800_53.description: Physically control and securely store digital and non-digital media.

nist_800_53.title: Media Transport
nist_800_53.description: Protect and control digital and non-digital media during transport outside of controlled areas.

nist_800_53.title: Media Sanitization
nist_800_53.description: Sanitize information system media prior to disposal, release out of organizational control, or release for reuse.

nist_800_53.title: Media Use
nist_800_53.description: Restrict or prohibit the use of certain types of media.

nist_800_53.title: Physical and Environmental Protection
nist_800_53.description: Limit physical access to equipment and the facility while protecting against environmental hazards.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate physical and environmental protection policy.

nist_800_53.title: Physical Access Authorizations
nist_800_53.description: Develop, approve, and maintain a list of individuals with authorized physical access.

nist_800_53.title: Physical Access Control
nist_800_53.description: Enforce physical access authorizations; control ingress and egress points.

nist_800_53.title: Access Control for Transmission
nist_800_53.description: Control physical access to system distribution and transmission lines.

nist_800_53.title: Access Control for Output Devices
nist_800_53.description: Control physical access to output devices to prevent unauthorized access to output.

nist_800_53.title: Monitoring Physical Access
nist_800_53.description: Monitor physical access to the facility to detect and respond to physical security incidents.

nist_800_53.title: Visitor Control
nist_800_53.description: Control physical access by visitors including escort requirements.

nist_800_53.title: Visitor Access Records
nist_800_53.description: Maintain visitor access records to the facility.

nist_800_53.title: Power Equipment and Cabling
nist_800_53.description: Protect power equipment and power cabling from damage and destruction.

nist_800_53.title: Emergency Shutoff
nist_800_53.description: Provide the capability of shutting off power to the information system in emergency situations.

nist_800_53.title: Emergency Power
nist_800_53.description: Provide a short-term uninterruptible power supply to facilitate an orderly shutdown.

nist_800_53.title: Emergency Lighting
nist_800_53.description: Employ and maintain automatic emergency lighting for the information system.

nist_800_53.title: Fire Protection
nist_800_53.description: Employ and maintain fire detection and suppression devices.

nist_800_53.title: Environmental Controls
nist_800_53.description: Maintain temperature and humidity levels within acceptable limits.

nist_800_53.title: Water Damage Protection
nist_800_53.description: Protect the information system from damage resulting from water leakage.

nist_800_53.title: Delivery and Removal
nist_800_53.description: Authorize, monitor, and control information system-related items entering and exiting the facility.

nist_800_53.title: Planning
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, update, and implement security plans and related planning documents.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate planning policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: System Security and Privacy Plans
nist_800_53.description: Develop security and privacy plans for the system, including system boundaries.

nist_800_53.title: Rules of Behavior
nist_800_53.description: Establish and make readily available to individuals the rules that describe their responsibilities.

nist_800_53.title: Concept of Operations
nist_800_53.description: Develop a Concept of Operations (CONOPS) for the system.

nist_800_53.title: Security and Privacy Architectures
nist_800_53.description: Develop security and privacy architectures for the system using a defense-in-depth approach.

nist_800_53.title: Central Management
nist_800_53.description: Centralize the management of security controls for the information system.

nist_800_53.title: Program Management
nist_800_53.description: Manage information security at the organizational level; independent of particular systems.

nist_800_53.title: Information Security Program Plan
nist_800_53.description: Develop and disseminate an organization-wide information security program plan.

nist_800_53.title: Information Security Program Leadership Role
nist_800_53.description: Appoint a senior agency official to coordinate the security program.

nist_800_53.title: Information Security Program Resources
nist_800_53.description: Ensure that capital planning and investment requests include security resources.

nist_800_53.title: Plan of Action and Milestones Process
nist_800_53.description: Implement a process for ensuring that plans of action and milestones are remediated.

nist_800_53.title: System Inventory
nist_800_53.description: Develop and update an inventory of information systems.

nist_800_53.title: Measures of Performance
nist_800_53.description: Develop, monitor, and report on information security measures of performance.

nist_800_53.title: Enterprise Architecture
nist_800_53.description: Develop an enterprise architecture with consideration for information security.

nist_800_53.title: Critical Infrastructure Plan
nist_800_53.description: Address information security issues in the development and documentation of critical infrastructure plans.

nist_800_53.title: Risk Management Strategy
nist_800_53.description: Develop a comprehensive strategy to manage risk to organizational operations and assets.

nist_800_53.title: Authorization Process
nist_800_53.description: Manage the security and privacy state of the system through authorization processes.

nist_800_53.title: Mission/Business Process Definition
nist_800_53.description: Define mission and business processes with consideration for information security.

nist_800_53.title: Security and Privacy Workforce
nist_800_53.description: Establish a security and privacy workforce development and improvement program.

nist_800_53.title: Testing, Training, and Monitoring
nist_800_53.description: Implement testing, training, and monitoring plan for security controls.

nist_800_53.title: Security and Privacy Groups and Associations
nist_800_53.description: Establish and institutionalize contact with groups and associations within the security and privacy fields.

nist_800_53.title: Threat Awareness Program
nist_800_53.description: Implement a threat awareness program that includes cross-organization information sharing.

nist_800_53.title: Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information on External Systems
nist_800_53.description: Protect controlled unclassified information on external systems.

nist_800_53.title: Privacy Program Plan
nist_800_53.description: Develop and implement an organization-wide privacy program plan.

nist_800_53.title: Dissemination of Privacy Program Information
nist_800_53.description: Maintain a central repository of privacy program information.

nist_800_53.title: Accounting of Disclosures
nist_800_53.description: Develop and maintain an accounting of disclosures of personally identifiable information.

nist_800_53.title: Personally Identifiable Information Quality Management
nist_800_53.description: Develop and implement policies to improve the quality of PII.

nist_800_53.title: Minimization of Personally Identifiable Information Used in Testing
nist_800_53.description: Minimize the use of PII for testing, training, and research.

nist_800_53.title: Privacy Reporting
nist_800_53.description: Develop and maintain internal privacy reports that are shared with senior management.

nist_800_53.title: Risk Framing
nist_800_53.description: Identify and document assumptions affecting risk assessments and risk responses.

nist_800_53.title: Risk Management Program Leadership Roles
nist_800_53.description: Assign and maintain assigned roles for risk management.

nist_800_53.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Strategy
nist_800_53.description: Develop an organization-wide strategy for managing supply chain risks.

nist_800_53.title: Continuous Monitoring Strategy
nist_800_53.description: Develop an organization-wide strategy for continuous monitoring.

nist_800_53.title: Purple Team Testing
nist_800_53.description: Employ the results of purple team exercises to improve security posture.

nist_800_53.title: Personnel Security
nist_800_53.description: Ensure personnel occupying positions of responsibility are trustworthy and meet security criteria.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate personnel security policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Position Risk Designation
nist_800_53.description: Assign a risk designation to all organizational positions.

nist_800_53.title: Personnel Screening
nist_800_53.description: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to the information system.

nist_800_53.title: Personnel Termination
nist_800_53.description: Upon termination of individual employment, terminate information system access.

nist_800_53.title: Personnel Transfer
nist_800_53.description: Review and confirm ongoing operational need for logical and physical access authorizations.

nist_800_53.title: Access Agreements
nist_800_53.description: Develop and document access agreements for organizational information systems.

nist_800_53.title: External Personnel Security
nist_800_53.description: Ensure external providers meet the same personnel security requirements as organizational personnel.

nist_800_53.title: Personnel Sanctions
nist_800_53.description: Employ a formal sanctions process for individuals failing to comply with security policies.

nist_800_53.title: Position Descriptions
nist_800_53.description: Incorporate security and privacy roles and responsibilities into organizational position descriptions.

nist_800_53.title: PII Processing and Transparency
nist_800_53.description: Manage the processing of personally identifiable information (PII) with transparency and accountability.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate PII processing and transparency policy.

nist_800_53.title: Authority to Process PII
nist_800_53.description: Document the authority to process personally identifiable information.

nist_800_53.title: PII Processing Purposes
nist_800_53.description: Identify the purpose(s) for which PII is collected, used, maintained, and shared.

nist_800_53.title: Consent
nist_800_53.description: Obtain consent from individuals prior to any new use or sharing of their PII.

nist_800_53.title: Privacy Notice
nist_800_53.description: Provide a privacy notice that is publicly available and describes PII processing activities.

nist_800_53.title: System of Records Notice
nist_800_53.description: For systems containing PII processed on behalf of a federal agency, publish SORNs.

nist_800_53.title: Specific Categories of PII
nist_800_53.description: Apply specific conditions or protections for special categories of PII.

nist_800_53.title: Risk Assessment
nist_800_53.description: Assess the risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals from operation of information systems.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate risk assessment policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Security Categorization
nist_800_53.description: Categorize information and the information system in accordance with applicable laws.

nist_800_53.title: Risk Assessment
nist_800_53.description: Conduct a risk assessment, including an assessment of the likelihood and magnitude of harm.

nist_800_53.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
nist_800_53.description: Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the information system and hosted applications.

nist_800_53.title: Risk Response
nist_800_53.description: Respond to findings from security and privacy assessments, audits, and continuous monitoring.

nist_800_53.title: Criticality Analysis
nist_800_53.description: Identify critical system components and functions by performing a criticality analysis.

nist_800_53.title: Threat Hunting
nist_800_53.description: Establish a threat hunting capability based on analysis of threat intelligence and system data.

nist_800_53.title: System and Services Acquisition
nist_800_53.description: Allocate resources for the acquisition and deployment of information systems and services.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate system and services acquisition policy.

nist_800_53.title: Allocation of Resources
nist_800_53.description: Determine, document, and allocate resources for protecting the system.

nist_800_53.title: System Development Life Cycle
nist_800_53.description: Manage the information system using a system development life cycle methodology.

nist_800_53.title: Acquisition Process
nist_800_53.description: Include security and privacy requirements in the acquisition contract for the system.

nist_800_53.title: System Documentation
nist_800_53.description: Obtain administrator documentation and user documentation for the information system.

nist_800_53.title: Security and Privacy Engineering Principles
nist_800_53.description: Apply information security and privacy engineering principles in the specification, design, development, and implementation of information systems.

nist_800_53.title: External System Services
nist_800_53.description: Require that providers of external system services comply with organizational security requirements.

nist_800_53.title: Developer Configuration Management
nist_800_53.description: Require the developer to perform configuration management during system development.

nist_800_53.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation
nist_800_53.description: Require the developer to create and implement a security testing and evaluation plan.

nist_800_53.title: Development Process, Standards, and Tools
nist_800_53.description: Require the developer to follow a documented development process.

nist_800_53.title: Unsupported System Components
nist_800_53.description: Replace information system components when support is no longer available.

nist_800_53.title: System and Communications Protection
nist_800_53.description: Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information at rest, in transit, and in use.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate system and communications protection policy.

nist_800_53.title: Separation of System and User Functionality
nist_800_53.description: Separate user functionality from system management functionality.

nist_800_53.title: Security Function Isolation
nist_800_53.description: Isolate security functions from non-security functions.

nist_800_53.title: Information in Shared System Resources
nist_800_53.description: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

nist_800_53.title: Denial-of-Service Protection
nist_800_53.description: Protect against or limit the effects of denial-of-service attacks.

nist_800_53.title: Boundary Protection
nist_800_53.description: Monitor and control communications at the external boundary of the system.

nist_800_53.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
nist_800_53.description: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of transmitted information.

nist_800_53.title: Network Disconnect
nist_800_53.description: Terminate the network connection associated with a communications session at the end of the session.

nist_800_53.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
nist_800_53.description: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for required cryptography.

nist_800_53.title: Cryptographic Protection
nist_800_53.description: Determine and document the required cryptographic protection for the information system.

nist_800_53.title: Collaborative Computing Devices and Applications
nist_800_53.description: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and applications.

nist_800_53.title: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
nist_800_53.description: Issue public key certificates or obtain PKI certificates from an approved service provider.

nist_800_53.title: Mobile Code
nist_800_53.description: Define acceptable and unacceptable mobile code and mobile code technologies.

nist_800_53.title: Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Authoritative Source)
nist_800_53.description: Provide additional data origin authentication and integrity verification artifacts.

nist_800_53.title: Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Recursive or Caching Resolver)
nist_800_53.description: Request and perform data origin authentication and data integrity verification.

nist_800_53.title: Architecture and Provisioning for Name/Address Resolution Service
nist_800_53.description: Ensure name/address resolution services are fault-tolerant and implement security controls.

nist_800_53.title: Session Authenticity
nist_800_53.description: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions.

nist_800_53.title: Protection of Information at Rest
nist_800_53.description: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of information at rest.

nist_800_53.title: Heterogeneity
nist_800_53.description: Employ a diverse set of information technologies to limit exploitation risk.

nist_800_53.title: Covert Channel Analysis
nist_800_53.description: Perform covert channel analysis to identify and reduce information leakage.

nist_800_53.title: Information System Partitioning
nist_800_53.description: Partition the information system into components residing in separate physical domains or environments.

nist_800_53.title: Non-Modifiable Executable Programs
nist_800_53.description: Load and execute the operating environment from hardware-enforced, non-modifiable storage.

nist_800_53.title: Distributed Processing and Storage
nist_800_53.description: Distribute processing and storage across multiple physical locations.

nist_800_53.title: Operations Security
nist_800_53.description: Employ operations security to protect key organizational information.

nist_800_53.title: Process Isolation
nist_800_53.description: Maintain a separate execution domain for each executing process.

nist_800_53.title: Wireless Link Protection
nist_800_53.description: Protect external and internal wireless links from signal parameter attacks.

nist_800_53.title: Sensor Capability and Data
nist_800_53.description: Prohibit the remote activation or use of environmental sensing capabilities.

nist_800_53.title: Usage Restrictions
nist_800_53.description: Establish usage restrictions for system components that have no wireless capabilities.

nist_800_53.title: System Time Synchronization
nist_800_53.description: Synchronize system clocks within and between systems and with authoritative time sources.

nist_800_53.title: Cross-Domain Policy Enforcement
nist_800_53.description: Enforce policy on data to be transferred across security domains.

nist_800_53.title: Alternate Communications Paths
nist_800_53.description: Provide alternate communications paths that are physically separate.

nist_800_53.title: Hardware-Enforced Separation and Policy Enforcement
nist_800_53.description: Implement hardware-enforced separation and policy enforcement mechanisms.

nist_800_53.title: Software-Enforced Separation and Policy Enforcement
nist_800_53.description: Implement software-enforced separation and policy enforcement mechanisms.

nist_800_53.title: Hardware-Based Protection
nist_800_53.description: Use hardware-based protection techniques to protect the system from unauthorized access or modification.

nist_800_53.title: System and Information Integrity
nist_800_53.description: Identify, report, and correct information system flaws in a timely manner; protect the integrity of information.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate system and information integrity policy.

nist_800_53.title: Flaw Remediation
nist_800_53.description: Identify, report, and correct information system flaws.

nist_800_53.title: Malicious Code Protection
nist_800_53.description: Implement malicious code protection mechanisms at system entry and exit points.

nist_800_53.title: System Monitoring
nist_800_53.description: Monitor the information system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

nist_800_53.title: Security Alerts, Advisories, and Directives
nist_800_53.description: Receive information system security alerts, advisories, and directives on an ongoing basis.

nist_800_53.title: Security and Privacy Function Verification
nist_800_53.description: Verify the correct operation of security and privacy functions.

nist_800_53.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
nist_800_53.description: Employ integrity verification mechanisms to detect unauthorized changes.

nist_800_53.title: Spam Protection
nist_800_53.description: Employ spam protection mechanisms at system entry and exit points.

nist_800_53.title: Information Input Validation
nist_800_53.description: Check the validity of information inputs.

nist_800_53.title: Error Handling
nist_800_53.description: Generate error messages that provide information necessary for corrective actions.

nist_800_53.title: Information Management and Retention
nist_800_53.description: Manage and retain information within the system.

nist_800_53.title: Predictable Failure Prevention
nist_800_53.description: Employ a means to prevent predictable failures of system components.

nist_800_53.title: Non-Persistence
nist_800_53.description: Implement the capability to implement non-persistent information system components and services.

nist_800_53.title: Memory Protection
nist_800_53.description: Implement protections to prevent unauthorized code execution.

nist_800_53.title: Fail-Safe Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Implement procedures to ensure the system fails securely when a failure condition is detected.

nist_800_53.title: Supply Chain Risk Management
nist_800_53.description: Manage the risks associated with the products and services that information systems rely on.

nist_800_53.title: Policy and Procedures
nist_800_53.description: Develop, document, and disseminate supply chain risk management policy.

nist_800_53.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
nist_800_53.description: Develop a plan for managing supply chain risks associated with the IT supply chain.

nist_800_53.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
nist_800_53.description: Establish a process to identify and address weaknesses in the supply chain.

nist_800_53.title: Provenance
nist_800_53.description: Document and monitor the provenance of system components.

nist_800_53.title: Acquisition Strategies, Tools, and Methods
nist_800_53.description: Employ acquisition strategies that protect against, mitigate, and manage supply chain risk.

nist_800_53.title: Supplier Assessments and Reviews
nist_800_53.description: Assess and review the supply chain-related risks associated with suppliers.

nist_800_53.title: Supply Chain Operations Security
nist_800_53.description: Employ operations security to protect supply chain-related information.

nist_800_53.title: Notification Agreements
nist_800_53.description: Establish agreements and procedures with entities involved in the supply chain.

nist_800_53.title: Tamper Resistance and Detection
nist_800_53.description: Employ tamper protection for system components during the system development life cycle.

nist_800_53.title: Inspection of Systems or Components
nist_800_53.description: Inspect information systems and components to detect tampering.

nist_800_53.title: Component Authenticity
nist_800_53.description: Develop and implement anti-counterfeit policy and procedures.

nist_800_53.title: Component Disposal
nist_800_53.description: Dispose of system components using approved techniques and methods.

nist_csf.title: Govern
nist_csf.description: Establish and monitor the organization's cybersecurity risk management strategy, expectations, and policy.

nist_csf.title: Organizational Context
nist_csf.description: The circumstances — mission, stakeholder expectations, dependencies, and legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements — surrounding the organization's cybersecurity risk management decisions.

nist_csf.title: GV.OC-01
nist_csf.description: The organizational mission is understood and informs cybersecurity risk management.

nist_csf.title: GV.OC-02
nist_csf.description: Internal and external stakeholders are understood, their needs and expectations regarding cybersecurity risk management are identified, and their input is received and considered.

nist_csf.title: GV.OC-03
nist_csf.description: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding cybersecurity — including privacy and civil liberties obligations — are understood and managed.

nist_csf.title: GV.OC-04
nist_csf.description: Critical objectives, capabilities, and services that external stakeholders depend on or expect from the organization are understood and communicated.

nist_csf.title: GV.OC-05
nist_csf.description: Outcomes, capabilities, and services that the organization depends on from others are understood and communicated.

nist_csf.title: Risk Management Strategy
nist_csf.description: The organization's priorities, constraints, risk tolerance and appetite statements, and assumptions supporting operational risk decisions.

nist_csf.title: GV.RM-01
nist_csf.description: Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders.

nist_csf.title: GV.RM-02
nist_csf.description: Risk appetite and risk tolerance statements are established, communicated, and maintained.

nist_csf.title: GV.RM-03
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity risk management activities and outcomes are included in enterprise risk management processes.

nist_csf.title: GV.RM-04
nist_csf.description: Strategic direction that describes appropriate risk response options is established and communicated.

nist_csf.title: GV.RM-05
nist_csf.description: Lines of communication across the organization are established for cybersecurity risk management.

nist_csf.title: GV.RM-06
nist_csf.description: A standardized method for calculating, evaluating, and prioritizing cybersecurity risk is established and communicated.

nist_csf.title: GV.RM-07
nist_csf.description: Strategic opportunities (positive risks) are identified and appropriate actions are taken.

nist_csf.title: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities to foster accountability, performance assessment, and continuous improvement.

nist_csf.title: GV.RR-01
nist_csf.description: Organizational leadership responsibility for cybersecurity risk is established, communicated, and overseen.

nist_csf.title: GV.RR-02
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity leadership roles and responsibilities are established, communicated, and exercised.

nist_csf.title: GV.RR-03
nist_csf.description: Adequate resources commensurate with the cybersecurity risk strategy are established and communicated.

nist_csf.title: GV.RR-04
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity is included in human resources practices, including security screening, onboarding, and offboarding.

nist_csf.title: Policy
nist_csf.description: Organizational cybersecurity policy is established, communicated, and enforced.

nist_csf.title: GV.PO-01
nist_csf.description: A policy for managing cybersecurity risks is developed based on organizational context and informed by applicable requirements.

nist_csf.title: GV.PO-02
nist_csf.description: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is communicated to internal and external stakeholders.

nist_csf.title: Oversight
nist_csf.description: Results of organization-wide cybersecurity risk management activities and performance are used to inform, improve, and adjust the risk management strategy.

nist_csf.title: GV.OV-01
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity risk management strategy outcomes are reviewed by organizational leadership to inform strategic decision-making.

nist_csf.title: GV.OV-02
nist_csf.description: The performance of the cybersecurity risk management strategy is evaluated and adjusted.

nist_csf.title: GV.OV-03
nist_csf.description: Organizational cybersecurity risk management performance is evaluated and communicated to senior leadership.

nist_csf.title: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
nist_csf.description: Cyber supply chain risk management processes are identified, established, managed, monitored, and improved by organizational stakeholders.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-01
nist_csf.description: A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program, strategy, objectives, policies, and processes are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-02
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities for suppliers, customers, and partners are established and communicated.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-03
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management is integrated into cybersecurity and enterprise risk management, risk assessment, and improvement processes.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-04
nist_csf.description: Suppliers with known criticality are prioritized and appropriate risk management actions are taken.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-05
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management planning is performed as part of the acquisition process.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-06
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management activities and performance are included in cybersecurity and enterprise risk management.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-07
nist_csf.description: Changes in suppliers during relationships are managed, and the risk of the change is assessed.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-08
nist_csf.description: Relevant internal and external stakeholders receive cybersecurity supply chain risk management information.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-09
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management plans include provisions for determining a disposition for data, services, and products when the relationship with a supplier is terminated.

nist_csf.title: GV.SC-10
nist_csf.description: Supply chain security is included in security awareness and training and in role-based training programs.

nist_csf.title: Identify
nist_csf.description: Determine the current cybersecurity risks to the organization.

nist_csf.title: Asset Management
nist_csf.description: The data, personnel, devices, systems, and facilities that enable the organization to achieve business purposes are identified and managed consistent with their relative importance.

nist_csf.title: ID.AM-01
nist_csf.description: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained.

nist_csf.title: ID.AM-02
nist_csf.description: Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained.

nist_csf.title: ID.AM-03
nist_csf.description: Representations of the organization's authorized network communication and internal and external network data flows are maintained.

nist_csf.title: ID.AM-04
nist_csf.description: Inventories of services provided by suppliers are maintained.

nist_csf.title: ID.AM-05
nist_csf.description: Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, resources, and impact on the mission.

nist_csf.title: ID.AM-07
nist_csf.description: Inventories of data and corresponding metadata for prioritized assets are maintained.

nist_csf.title: ID.AM-08
nist_csf.description: Systems, hardware, software, services, and data are managed throughout their life cycles.

nist_csf.title: Risk Assessment
nist_csf.description: The cybersecurity risk to the organization, assets, and individuals is understood by the organization.

nist_csf.title: ID.RA-01
nist_csf.description: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded.

nist_csf.title: ID.RA-02
nist_csf.description: Cyber threat intelligence is received and analyzed from information sharing forums and sources.

nist_csf.title: ID.RA-03
nist_csf.description: Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and recorded.

nist_csf.title: ID.RA-04
nist_csf.description: Potential impacts and likelihoods of threats materializing are identified and recorded.

nist_csf.title: ID.RA-05
nist_csf.description: Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to understand inherent risk and inform risk response prioritization.

nist_csf.title: ID.RA-06
nist_csf.description: Risk responses are chosen from the available options, prioritized, planned, tracked, and communicated.

nist_csf.title: Improvement
nist_csf.description: Improvements to organizational cybersecurity risk management processes, procedures, and activities are identified across all CSF Functions.

nist_csf.title: ID.IM-01
nist_csf.description: Improvements are identified from evaluations.

nist_csf.title: ID.IM-02
nist_csf.description: Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties.

nist_csf.title: ID.IM-03
nist_csf.description: Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities.

nist_csf.title: ID.IM-04
nist_csf.description: Incident response plans and other risk management plans are evaluated and recommendations are communicated to leadership.

nist_csf.title: Protect
nist_csf.description: Use safeguards to prevent or reduce cybersecurity risk.

nist_csf.title: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
nist_csf.description: Access to physical and logical assets is limited to authorized users, services, and hardware and managed commensurate with the assessed risk.

nist_csf.title: PR.AA-01
nist_csf.description: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization.

nist_csf.title: PR.AA-02
nist_csf.description: Identities are proofed and bound to credentials based on the context of interactions.

nist_csf.title: PR.AA-03
nist_csf.description: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated.

nist_csf.title: PR.AA-04
nist_csf.description: Identity assertions are protected, conveyed, and verified.

nist_csf.title: PR.AA-05
nist_csf.description: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties.

nist_csf.title: PR.AA-06
nist_csf.description: Physical access to assets is managed, monitored, and enforced commensurate with risk.

nist_csf.title: Awareness and Training
nist_csf.description: The organization's personnel are provided cybersecurity awareness and training so they can perform their cybersecurity-related tasks.

nist_csf.title: PR.AT-01
nist_csf.description: All users are informed and trained about their security and privacy responsibilities.

nist_csf.title: PR.AT-02
nist_csf.description: Individuals in specialized roles receive role-based cybersecurity training.

nist_csf.title: Data Security
nist_csf.description: Data is managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information.

nist_csf.title: PR.DS-01
nist_csf.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected.

nist_csf.title: PR.DS-02
nist_csf.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected.

nist_csf.title: PR.DS-10
nist_csf.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected.

nist_csf.title: PR.DS-11
nist_csf.description: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested.

nist_csf.title: Platform Security
nist_csf.description: The hardware, software (e.g., firmware, operating systems, applications), and services of physical and virtual platforms are managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy.

nist_csf.title: PR.PS-01
nist_csf.description: Configuration management practices are established and applied.

nist_csf.title: PR.PS-02
nist_csf.description: Software is maintained and replaced in accordance with risk.

nist_csf.title: PR.PS-03
nist_csf.description: Configuration change control is implemented.

nist_csf.title: PR.PS-04
nist_csf.description: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring.

nist_csf.title: PR.PS-05
nist_csf.description: Installation and execution of unauthorized software are prevented.

nist_csf.title: PR.PS-06
nist_csf.description: Secure software development practices are integrated and their performance is monitored throughout the software development life cycle.

nist_csf.title: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
nist_csf.description: Security architectures are managed with the organization's risk strategy to protect asset confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

nist_csf.title: PR.IR-01
nist_csf.description: Networks and environments are segmented based on risk.

nist_csf.title: PR.IR-02
nist_csf.description: Network resilience and redundancy are designed commensurate with risk criticality.

nist_csf.title: PR.IR-03
nist_csf.description: Adequate resource capacity to ensure availability is maintained.

nist_csf.title: PR.IR-04
nist_csf.description: Adequate resource capacity is available for critical infrastructure.

nist_csf.title: PR.IR-05
nist_csf.description: Redundancy for critical systems is implemented based on risk.

nist_csf.title: Detect
nist_csf.description: Find and analyze possible cybersecurity attacks and compromises.

nist_csf.title: Continuous Monitoring
nist_csf.description: Assets are monitored to find anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events.

nist_csf.title: DE.CM-01
nist_csf.description: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events.

nist_csf.title: DE.CM-02
nist_csf.description: The physical environment is monitored to find potentially adverse events.

nist_csf.title: DE.CM-03
nist_csf.description: Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events.

nist_csf.title: DE.CM-06
nist_csf.description: External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events.

nist_csf.title: DE.CM-09
nist_csf.description: Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events.

nist_csf.title: Adverse Event Analysis
nist_csf.description: Anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events are analyzed to characterize the events and detect cybersecurity incidents.

nist_csf.title: DE.AE-02
nist_csf.description: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities.

nist_csf.title: DE.AE-03
nist_csf.description: Information is correlated from multiple sources.

nist_csf.title: DE.AE-04
nist_csf.description: The estimated impact and scope of adverse events are understood.

nist_csf.title: DE.AE-06
nist_csf.description: Information on adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools.

nist_csf.title: DE.AE-07
nist_csf.description: Cyber threat intelligence and other contextual information are integrated into the analysis.

nist_csf.title: DE.AE-08
nist_csf.description: Incidents are declared when adverse events meet the defined incident criteria.

nist_csf.title: Respond
nist_csf.description: Take action regarding a detected cybersecurity incident.

nist_csf.title: Incident Management
nist_csf.description: Responses to detected cybersecurity incidents are managed.

nist_csf.title: RS.MA-01
nist_csf.description: The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared.

nist_csf.title: RS.MA-02
nist_csf.description: Incident reports are triaged and validated.

nist_csf.title: RS.MA-03
nist_csf.description: Incidents are categorized and prioritized.

nist_csf.title: RS.MA-04
nist_csf.description: Incidents are escalated or elevated as needed.

nist_csf.title: RS.MA-05
nist_csf.description: The criteria for initiating incident recovery are defined and agreed to.

nist_csf.title: Incident Analysis
nist_csf.description: Investigations are conducted to ensure effective response and support forensics and recovery activities.

nist_csf.title: RS.AN-03
nist_csf.description: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident.

nist_csf.title: RS.AN-06
nist_csf.description: Actions performed during an investigation are recorded, and the records' integrity and provenance are preserved.

nist_csf.title: RS.AN-07
nist_csf.description: Incident data and metadata are collected, and their integrity and provenance are preserved.

nist_csf.title: RS.AN-08
nist_csf.description: An incident's magnitude is estimated and validated.

nist_csf.title: Incident Response Reporting and Communication
nist_csf.description: Response activities are coordinated with internal and external stakeholders as required by laws, regulations, or policy.

nist_csf.title: RS.CO-02
nist_csf.description: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents.

nist_csf.title: RS.CO-03
nist_csf.description: Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders consistent with response plans and information sharing agreements.

nist_csf.title: RS.CO-04
nist_csf.description: Reputation is managed through defined coordinating structures.

nist_csf.title: RS.CO-05
nist_csf.description: Voluntary information sharing occurs with external stakeholders to achieve broader cybersecurity situational awareness.

nist_csf.title: Incident Mitigation
nist_csf.description: Activities are performed to prevent expansion of an event and to mitigate the effects of an incident.

nist_csf.title: RS.MI-01
nist_csf.description: Incidents are contained.

nist_csf.title: RS.MI-02
nist_csf.description: Incidents are eradicated.

nist_csf.title: RS.MI-03
nist_csf.description: Incident-related forensic evidence is handled in accordance with policy.

nist_csf.title: Recover
nist_csf.description: Restore assets and operations that were impacted by a cybersecurity incident.

nist_csf.title: Incident Recovery Plan Execution
nist_csf.description: Restoration activities are performed to ensure operational availability of systems and services affected by cybersecurity incidents.

nist_csf.title: RC.RP-01
nist_csf.description: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated from the incident response process or in response to a disaster.

nist_csf.title: RC.RP-02
nist_csf.description: Recovery actions are selected, scoped, prioritized, and performed.

nist_csf.title: RC.RP-03
nist_csf.description: The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before using them for restoration.

nist_csf.title: RC.RP-04
nist_csf.description: Critical mission functions and cybersecurity risk management are considered to establish post-incident operational norms.

nist_csf.title: RC.RP-05
nist_csf.description: The integrity of restored assets is verified, systems and services are restored, and normal operating status is confirmed.

nist_csf.title: RC.RP-06
nist_csf.description: The end of incident recovery is declared based on criteria, and incident-related documentation is completed.

nist_csf.title: Incident Recovery Communication
nist_csf.description: Restoration activities are coordinated with internal and external parties.

nist_csf.title: RC.CO-03
nist_csf.description: Recovery activities and progress in restoring operational capabilities are communicated to designated internal and external stakeholders.

nist_csf.title: RC.CO-04
nist_csf.description: Public updates on incident recovery are shared using approved messaging and methods.

cmmc.title: Access Control
cmmc.description: Limit information system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.1
cmmc.description: Limit system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other systems).

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.2
cmmc.description: Limit system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.3
cmmc.description: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.4
cmmc.description: Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.5
cmmc.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.6
cmmc.description: Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.7
cmmc.description: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.8
cmmc.description: Limit unsuccessful logon attempts.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.9
cmmc.description: Provide privacy and security notices consistent with applicable CUI rules.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.10
cmmc.description: Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays to prevent access and viewing of data after a period of inactivity.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.11
cmmc.description: Terminate (automatically) a user session after a defined condition.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.12
cmmc.description: Monitor and control remote access sessions.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.13
cmmc.description: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.14
cmmc.description: Route remote access via managed access control points.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.15
cmmc.description: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands and remote access to security-relevant information.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.16
cmmc.description: Authorize wireless access prior to allowing such connections.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.17
cmmc.description: Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.18
cmmc.description: Control connection of mobile devices.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.19
cmmc.description: Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.20
cmmc.description: Verify and control/limit connections to and use of external systems.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.21
cmmc.description: Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems.

cmmc.title: AC.L2-3.1.22
cmmc.description: Control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible systems.

cmmc.title: Awareness and Training
cmmc.description: Ensure managers, systems administrators, and users are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies and procedures.

cmmc.title: AT.L2-3.2.1
cmmc.description: Ensure that managers, system administrators, and users are made aware of security risks associated with their activities.

cmmc.title: AT.L2-3.2.2
cmmc.description: Ensure that personnel are trained to carry out their assigned information security-related duties.

cmmc.title: AT.L2-3.2.3
cmmc.description: Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat.

cmmc.title: Audit and Accountability
cmmc.description: Create, protect, and retain system audit records to enable monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.1
cmmc.description: Create and retain system audit logs and records to enable monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.2
cmmc.description: Ensure that the actions of individual system users can be uniquely traced to those users.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.3
cmmc.description: Review and update logged events.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.4
cmmc.description: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.5
cmmc.description: Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.6
cmmc.description: Provide audit record reduction and report generation to support on-demand analysis and reporting.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.7
cmmc.description: Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.8
cmmc.description: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

cmmc.title: AU.L2-3.3.9
cmmc.description: Limit management of audit logging functionality to a subset of privileged users.

cmmc.title: Security Assessment
cmmc.description: Periodically assess the security controls in organizational systems to determine if the controls are effective.

cmmc.title: CA.L2-3.12.1
cmmc.description: Periodically assess the security controls to determine if the controls are effective in their application.

cmmc.title: CA.L2-3.12.2
cmmc.description: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities.

cmmc.title: CA.L2-3.12.3
cmmc.description: Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of the controls.

cmmc.title: CA.L2-3.12.4
cmmc.description: Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans.

cmmc.title: Configuration Management
cmmc.description: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems throughout the system development life cycle.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.1
cmmc.description: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.2
cmmc.description: Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.3
cmmc.description: Track, review, approve or disapprove, and log changes to organizational systems.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.4
cmmc.description: Analyze the security impact of changes prior to implementation.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.5
cmmc.description: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.6
cmmc.description: Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring systems to provide only essential capabilities.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.7
cmmc.description: Restrict, disable, or prevent the use of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.8
cmmc.description: Apply deny-by-exception (blacklisting) policy to prevent the use of unauthorized software or deny-all, permit-by-exception (whitelisting) policy to allow execution of authorized software.

cmmc.title: CM.L2-3.4.9
cmmc.description: Control and monitor user-installed software.

cmmc.title: Identification and Authentication
cmmc.description: Identify information system users, processes acting on behalf of users, or devices and authenticate those identities before granting access.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.1
cmmc.description: Identify system users, processes acting on behalf of users, and devices.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.2
cmmc.description: Authenticate (or verify) the identities of users, processes, or devices as a prerequisite to allowing access.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.3
cmmc.description: Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and for network access to non-privileged accounts.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.4
cmmc.description: Employ replay-resistant authentication mechanisms for network access to privileged and non-privileged accounts.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.5
cmmc.description: Prevent reuse of identifiers for a defined period.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.6
cmmc.description: Disable identifiers after a defined period of inactivity.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.7
cmmc.description: Enforce a minimum password complexity and change of characters when new passwords are created.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.8
cmmc.description: Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.9
cmmc.description: Allow temporary password use for system logons with an immediate change to a permanent password.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.10
cmmc.description: Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords.

cmmc.title: IA.L2-3.5.11
cmmc.description: Obscure feedback of authentication information.

cmmc.title: Incident Response
cmmc.description: Establish an operational incident handling capability that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities.

cmmc.title: IR.L2-3.6.1
cmmc.description: Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities.

cmmc.title: IR.L2-3.6.2
cmmc.description: Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities.

cmmc.title: IR.L2-3.6.3
cmmc.description: Test the organizational incident response capability.

cmmc.title: Maintenance
cmmc.description: Perform maintenance on organizational systems.

cmmc.title: MA.L2-3.7.1
cmmc.description: Perform maintenance on organizational systems.

cmmc.title: MA.L2-3.7.2
cmmc.description: Provide controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used to conduct system maintenance.

cmmc.title: MA.L2-3.7.3
cmmc.description: Ensure equipment removed for off-site maintenance is sanitized of any CUI.

cmmc.title: MA.L2-3.7.4
cmmc.description: Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code before use.

cmmc.title: MA.L2-3.7.5
cmmc.description: Require multifactor authentication to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions via external network connections.

cmmc.title: MA.L2-3.7.6
cmmc.description: Supervise the maintenance activities of maintenance personnel without required access authorization.

cmmc.title: Media Protection
cmmc.description: Protect system media containing CUI, limiting access to authorized users; sanitize or destroy before disposal or release for reuse.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.1
cmmc.description: Protect (i.e., physically control and securely store) system media containing CUI.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.2
cmmc.description: Limit access to CUI on system media to authorized users.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.3
cmmc.description: Sanitize or destroy system media containing CUI before disposal or release for reuse.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.4
cmmc.description: Mark media with necessary CUI markings and distribution limitations.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.5
cmmc.description: Control access to media containing CUI and maintain accountability during transport outside controlled areas.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.6
cmmc.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of CUI stored on digital media during transport.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.7
cmmc.description: Control the use of removable media on system components.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.8
cmmc.description: Prohibit the use of portable storage devices when such devices have no identifiable owner.

cmmc.title: MP.L2-3.8.9
cmmc.description: Protect the confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations.

cmmc.title: Physical Protection
cmmc.description: Limit physical access to organizational systems, equipment, and the respective operating environments to authorized individuals.

cmmc.title: PE.L2-3.10.1
cmmc.description: Limit physical access to organizational systems, equipment, and the respective operating environments to authorized individuals.

cmmc.title: PE.L2-3.10.2
cmmc.description: Protect and monitor the physical facility and support infrastructure for organizational systems.

cmmc.title: PE.L2-3.10.3
cmmc.description: Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity.

cmmc.title: PE.L2-3.10.4
cmmc.description: Maintain audit logs of physical access.

cmmc.title: PE.L2-3.10.5
cmmc.description: Control and manage physical access devices.

cmmc.title: PE.L2-3.10.6
cmmc.description: Enforce safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites.

cmmc.title: Risk Assessment
cmmc.description: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations resulting from the operation of organizational systems and processing of CUI.

cmmc.title: RA.L2-3.11.1
cmmc.description: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals resulting from the operation of systems and processing of CUI.

cmmc.title: RA.L2-3.11.2
cmmc.description: Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities are identified.

cmmc.title: RA.L2-3.11.3
cmmc.description: Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments.

cmmc.title: RA.L2-3.11.4
cmmc.description: Share information obtained from the vulnerability scanning with designated personnel throughout the organization.

cmmc.title: RA.L2-3.11.5
cmmc.description: Employ a means for evaluating cyber threat intelligence sources.

cmmc.title: RA.L2-3.11.6
cmmc.description: Establish and maintain a cyber threat hunting capability.

cmmc.title: RA.L2-3.11.7
cmmc.description: Develop a risk response strategy that includes defined courses of action for known risks.

cmmc.title: System and Communications Protection
cmmc.description: Monitor, control, and protect communications at the external and internal boundaries of organizational systems.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.1
cmmc.description: Monitor, control, and protect organizational communications at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.2
cmmc.description: Employ architectural designs, software development techniques, and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.3
cmmc.description: Separate user functionality from system management functionality.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.4
cmmc.description: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.5
cmmc.description: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.6
cmmc.description: Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.7
cmmc.description: Prevent remote devices from simultaneously establishing non-remote connections and communicating via some other connection.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.8
cmmc.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission unless protected by alternative physical safeguards.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.9
cmmc.description: Terminate network connections associated with communications sessions at the end of the sessions or after a defined period of inactivity.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.10
cmmc.description: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for cryptography employed in organizational systems.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.11
cmmc.description: Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.12
cmmc.description: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and provide indication of devices in use to users present at the device.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.13
cmmc.description: Control and monitor the use of mobile code.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.14
cmmc.description: Control and monitor the use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.15
cmmc.description: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions.

cmmc.title: SC.L2-3.13.16
cmmc.description: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest.

cmmc.title: System and Information Integrity
cmmc.description: Identify, report, and correct system flaws in a timely manner; protect against malicious code; and monitor system security alerts and advisories.

cmmc.title: SI.L2-3.14.1
cmmc.description: Identify, report, and correct system flaws in a timely manner.

cmmc.title: SI.L2-3.14.2
cmmc.description: Provide protection from malicious code at designated locations within organizational systems.

cmmc.title: SI.L2-3.14.3
cmmc.description: Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take appropriate actions in response.

cmmc.title: SI.L2-3.14.4
cmmc.description: Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available.

cmmc.title: SI.L2-3.14.5
cmmc.description: Perform periodic scans of organizational systems and real-time scans of files from external sources.

cmmc.title: SI.L2-3.14.6
cmmc.description: Monitor organizational systems for unauthorized personnel, connections, devices, and software.

cmmc.title: SI.L2-3.14.7
cmmc.description: Employ spam protection mechanisms.

cmmc.title: Personnel Security
cmmc.description: Ensure that individuals occupying positions of responsibility are trustworthy and meet established security criteria.

cmmc.title: PS.L2-3.9.2
cmmc.description: Ensure that organizational systems containing CUI are protected during and after personnel actions such as terminations and transfers.
